Looking at the ASCII row 32: `gwwcccccwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwww...`
The c's are at cols 11-15 (5 columns). Row 33: `gNNNwwwcwwwwwwww...` - the c is at col 14 only. This suggests the charcoal box's right edge is at col 14 on row 33, not col 15.
Hmm, the charcoal box seems to have been modified. Let me check if the W-box ever overlapped with it. Looking at the earlier state where the W-box was at rows 33-35, cols 17-19, the charcoal box was at cols 11-15. They didn't overlap.

```

35: gNNNwwwcwwwwwwww...

This is strange. It seems like the charcoal box's left wall was removed. Maybe this happened when the W-box moved through it? The overlap would be at col 11, rows 33-35, which is the left edge of the charcoal box.
So moving the W-box DOWN caused it to overlap with the charcoal box's left edge, and the charcoal box's left wall was removed/merged. This might be the intended behavior - the W-box "enters" the charcoal box by removing the wall.
